title: Wireless network reliabilty over relatively low-power protocols
abstract: Systems and methods relating to communication over relatively low power protocols by adding reliability functionality are presented. Sent messages having reliability functionality include an acknowledgment request flag that indicates that the message includes a request to acknowledge the request once received and an application payload that includes data for the message. The acknowledgment request flag causes responding devices to send an acknowledgment message to the sending device indicating that the sent message has been received.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A non-transitory, computer-readable medium having instructions stored thereon, which when executed, are configured to cause a processor to: generate a message to include reliability functionality to be sent over a relatively low power communication protocol, wherein the message comprises a message format comprising: an acknowledgment request flag that indicates that the message includes a request to acknowledge the message once received; and an application payload that includes data for the message; and transmit the message to a remote device. 2. The non-transitory, computer-readable medium of claim 1, wherein the instructions are configured to cause the processor to: at the time that the message is sent, start an acknowledgment timer that has a predetermined threshold after which point the instructions are configured to cause the processor to retransmit the message; determine if the acknowledgment timer has surpassed the predetermined threshold; and if the acknowledgment timer has surpassed the predetermined threshold, retransmit the message. 3. The non-transitory, computer-readable medium of claim 1, wherein the instructions are configured to cause the processor to: determine whether a retransmission limit has been reached for the message; and 

 if the retransmission limit has been reached, block retransmission of the message and notify of the failure to transmit the message. 4. The non-transitory, computer-readable medium of claim 1, wherein the instructions are configured to cause the processor to: receive a throttle communications message from the remote device, wherein the throttle communications message indicates that the remote device is currently out of resources to accept the message or is estimated to be out of resources to accept the message due to traffic congestion, the throttle communications message includes an indication of a delay duration; and after a period of time at least as long as the delay duration, retransmit the message. 5. The non-transitory, computer-readable medium, of claim 4, wherein the instructions are configured to cause the processor to: determine whether the indication of the delay duration in the delay delivery message is predetermined value; and if the indication of the delay duration in the delay delivery message is the predetermined value, retransmit the message substantially immediately. 6. The non-transitory, computer-readable medium, of claim 1, wherein the relatively low power communication protocol comprises a protocol that transmits packets of data as datagrams from the processor to one or more remote devices. 7. The non-transitory, computer-readable medium of claim 1, wherein the instructions are 

 configured to cause the processor to: at the time that the message is sent, start an acknowledgment timer that has a predetermined threshold after which point the instructions are configured to cause the processor to retransmit the message; receive a delay delivery message from a routing device, wherein the routing device is configured to route the message to the remote device from a transmitting device, wherein the delay delivery message indicates that the remote device is currently unable to receive the message and includes an indication of a delay duration; generate an adjusted threshold by adding the delay duration to the predetermined threshold; and determine if the acknowledgment timer has surpassed the adjusted threshold. 8. An electronic device comprising: a processor; memory storing instructions, which when executed, are configured to cause the processor to: receive, over a relatively low power communication protocol from a remote device, a message that includes reliability functionality in the message, wherein the message comprises a message format comprising: an acknowledgment request flag that indicates that the message includes a request to acknowledge the request once received; an acknowledgment identifier configured to identify acknowledgment messages pertaining to the message; and 

 an application payload that includes data for the message; and generate an acknowledgment message for the message, wherein the acknowledgment message includes an acknowledgment message identifier indicating that the acknowledgment message is an acknowledgment of the message. 9. The electronic device of claim 8, wherein the instructions are configured to cause the processor to: upon receipt of the message, start an acknowledgment timer; and delay transmission of the acknowledgment message until the acknowledgment timer has reached an acknowledgment threshold. 10. The electronic device of claim 9, wherein the instructions are configured to cause the processor to: before the acknowledgment timer reaches an acknowledgment threshold, determine whether an outgoing message has commands or data bound for the remote device; and if the outgoing message is bound for the remote device, include the acknowledgment message in the outgoing message; transmit the outgoing message including the acknowledgment message even before the acknowledgment timer reaches an acknowledgment threshold; and stop and reset the acknowledgment timer. 11. The electronic device of claim 9, wherein the acknowledgment threshold for the 

 acknowledgment timer is determined relative in size to a retransmission threshold for a retransmission timer used by the remote device that sent the message to determine when a retransmission of the message is to be transmitted if no acknowledgment has been received from the electronic device. 12. The electronic device of claim 9, wherein the acknowledgment threshold for the acknowledgment timer is determined as half of a retransmission threshold for a retransmission timer used by the remote device that sent the message to determine when a retransmission of the message is to be transmitted if no acknowledgment has been received from the electronic device. 13. The electronic device of the claim 8, wherein the instructions are configured to cause the processor to: determine if the message is a duplicate of a previously received message using a common message identifier present in the message and the previously received message; and sending the acknowledgment message as an acknowledgment for both the message and the previously received message. 14. The electronic device of the claim 8, wherein the instructions are configured to cause the processor to: determine whether the electronic device has buffer space to receive new messages based on traffic congestion; and if the electronic device has no buffer space to receive new messages based on traffic congestion, send a throttle flow command to the remote device to cause the remote device to pause 

 communications with the electronic device for a specified duration of time. 15. The electronic device of claim 9, wherein the instructions are configured to cause the processor to: before the acknowledgment timer reaches an acknowledgment threshold, determine if additional acknowledgments are available to be bundled with the additional pending acknowledgment messages; if additional acknowledgments are available to be bundled with the additional pending messages, bundle the acknowledgment messages into a single acknowledgment message; and transmit the single acknowledgment message even before the acknowledgment timer reaches an acknowledgment threshold; and stop and reset the acknowledgment timer. 16. An electronic device comprising: a processor; memory storing instructions, which when executed, are configured to cause the processor to: receive, over a relatively low power communication protocol from a remote device destined for a sleepy device connected to the electronic device, a message that includes reliability functionality in the message, wherein the message comprises a message format comprising: an acknowledgment request flag that indicates that the message includes a request to acknowledge the request once received; and 

 an application payload that includes data for the message; and determine whether the sleepy device is in a sleep mode; if the sleepy device is not in the sleep mode, transmit the message to the sleepy device; and if the sleepy device is in the sleep mode: transmit a delay delivery request to the remote device to delay retransmissions of the message until a period where the sleepy device is not in the sleep mode; and store the message as a held message to be delivered to the sleepy device when it awakens from the sleep mode. 17. The electronic device of claim 16, wherein the delay delivery request includes a value that reflects a sleep duration of the sleep mode for the sleepy device, wherein the delay delivery request adds the value to an retransmission threshold for an retransmission timer at the remote device to cause the remote device to delay retransmission of the message until the retransmission timer has reached a delayed threshold that is a combination of the retransmission threshold and the value, wherein the retransmission threshold is a value reflecting how often retransmissions are to be sent. 18. The electronic device of claim 17, wherein the instructions are configured to cause the processor to: receive a message poll from the sleepy device when the sleepy device awakens from the sleep mode; and if any held messages were received during the sleep mode of the sleepy device, transmitting 

 the held messages to the sleepy device. 19. The electronic device of claim 18, wherein the instructions are configured to cause the processor to, during the sleep mode of the sleepy device, determine if the message is a duplicate of a previously received message destined for the sleepy device using a common message identifier present in the message and the previously received message, wherein transmitting the held messages comprises sending a single copy of the message instead of duplicate held messages. 20. The electronic device of claim 16, wherein the instructions are configured to cause the processor to: receive an acknowledgment message for the message from the sleepy device destined for the remote device; and forward the acknowledgment message to the remote device from the sleepy device.
